input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — two tabs: Active and Archived -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — separate flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a .NET MAUI AppShell.xaml setup with:
1. A flyout menu with two sections: "Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. A Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for a ProductDetailsPage

This is a straightforward code generation request - no need to search the codebase. I'll provide the XAML and the code-behind.

data.content:
Here's the structure:

**Flyout menu:**
| Item | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| Dashboard | Single `ShellContent` → `DashboardPage` |
| Products | `Tab` with two `ShellContent` items → top tabs for **Active** / **Archived** |
| Settings | Single `ShellContent` → `SettingsPage` |

**Key points:**

- The `<Tab>` wrapper inside the Products `FlyoutItem` gives you the two top-tabs. Without it, each `ShellContent` would become its own flyout entry.
- `ContentTemplate` + `DataTemplate` enables **lazy loading** — pages are created only when navigated to.
- `ProductDetailsPage` is registered via `Routing.RegisterRoute` in the code-behind since it's not in the visual tree (it's a detail page you push onto the stack).

**Navigate to details from a list page:**

```csharp
// Pass a query parameter
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

Receive it with `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` on `ProductDetailsPage` or the `IQueryAttributable` interface.

You'll need to create the five page classes under a `Pages/` namespace: `DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, and `ProductDetailsPage`.
